title: Ronet Coorr
content:

{{Quote|Also continuing a warlike tradition is Senator Ronet Coorr of Iseno. His high gravity homeworld maintains a storied legacy of military pageantry through its mainly traditional honor guard. He is expected to vote for the creation of an Army.|Jacen Kodash on Ronet Coorr for HoloNet News|<ref name="HoloNet News Vol 531 56">{{HoloNetNewsWeb|56|news/13516_3.html|Loyalist Committee Split on Vote}}</ref>}}
'''Ronet Coorr''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] of a [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] [[Species/Legends|species]] who [[Senator/Legends|represented]] the planet of [[Iseno/Legends|Iseno]] in the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]] during the [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]].

==Biography==
===Iseno senator===
{{Quote|Is Narcassan deliberately trying to end his Intelligence career? He's chasing a phantom.|Ronet Coorr on Vyn Narcassan's discovery of a secret communication channel on Coruscant.|<ref name="HoloNet News Vol 531 52" />}}
The [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]]<ref name="CSWE" /> [[Gender/Legends|male]] Ronet Coorr was a [[Senator/Legends|senator]]<ref name="DB" /> [[Homeworld/Legends|from]] the high [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]]<ref name="CSWE" /> [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]]<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of Iseno.<ref name="CSWE" /> Coorr represented his homeworld in the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]]. He was close to the office of [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] and was a supporter of the proposed Republic Military initiative.<ref name="DB" /> In [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]],<ref name="HNN49date">The [[Great ReSynchronization|date]] 13:3:28 corresponds to [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] per the reasoning [[Template:YearMathGRS|here]].</ref> during the [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]], Palpatine formed his [[Loyalist Committee/Legends|Loyalist Committee]], a group of ten senators including Coorr, that served in diplomatic and strategic capacities whilst developing a six-month action plan to negotiate, engage [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist]] elements, and settle disputes. The formation and Coorr's involvement were reported on in the [[HoloNet News CoCo District Edition|CoCo District Edition]] of [[HoloNet News/Legends|HoloNet News]].<ref name="HoloNet News Vol 531 49" /> 

Later that year,<ref name="HNN52date">The [[Great ReSynchronization|date]] 13:4:18 corresponds to [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] per the reasoning [[Template:YearMathGRS|here]].</ref> Senior [[Senate Bureau of Intelligence]] official [[Vyn Narcassan]] alleged that an individual active in the Republic government was going through great lengths to hide something, using a communications channel that directly bypassed highly classified detection procedures. Coorr was highly skeptical of the allegation. In an article for HoloNet News, Coorr questioned if Narcassan was trying to end his intelligence career and said he was chasing a phantom.<ref name="HoloNet News Vol 531 52">{{HoloNetNewsWeb|52|news/13418_3.html|SBI Officer Warns of Conspiracy}}</ref>

Coorr later traveled to the planet [[Alsakan/Legends|Alsakan]] to attend the [[Refugee Relief Movement/Legends|Refugee Relief Movement]] [[Refugee Relief Movement fundraiser|fundraiser]], held within the [[Xenvaer Civic Auditorium]]. While there, the senator spent twelve [[Standard minute/Legends|minutes]] talking to the [[Journalist/Legends|journalist]] [[Dyslogia Twang]] about redirecting arterial traffic through industrial sectors. In their write-up about the event on HoloNet News, Twang described the talk as unfortunate, and that the redirecting talk left them wanting to open their arterial traffic on the laughably dated drapes at the event.<ref name="HoloNet News Vol 531 53">{{HoloNetNewsWeb|53|life/13425_1.html|SPECIAL FEATURE: Sightings by Twang}}</ref>

===Kenobi's broadcast===
[[File:KenobisReport-AOTC.png|thumb|right|400px|Ronet Coorr (between [[Ister Paddie/Legends|Ister Paddie]] and [[Jar Jar Binks/Legends|Jar Jar Binks]]) viewed Obi-Wan Kenobi's broadcast.]]
During an [[Local day/Legends|evening]]<ref name="Insider 60">{{InsiderCite|60|Inside Attack of the Clones|Inside ''Attack of the Clones''}}</ref> in 22 BBY,<ref name="AotC Date">''[[The New Essential Chronology]] dates the events of {{Film|II}} to [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]].</ref> Coorr was present in<ref name="AotC">{{Film|II}}</ref> [[Chancellor's Suite/Legends|Palpatine's office]] in the [[Republic Executive Building/Legends|Republic Executive Building]] on the planet<ref name="Complete Locations">[[Star Wars: Complete Locations (2005)|''Star Wars: Complete Locations'']]</ref> [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. Coorr and other senators watched<ref name="AotC" /> with grave concern<ref name="Insider 60" /> a holographic message of [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]]. Broadcasting from the planet [[Geonosis/Legends|Geonosis]], Kenobi reported on the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] delivering a [[Droid/Legends|droid]] army, and that [[Nute Gunray/Legends|Nute Gunray]], [[Viceroy of the Trade Federation/Legends|Viceroy]] of the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]], was behind the [[Plot to assassinate Senator Amidala/Legends|assassination attempts]] on [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]]. Kenobi then continued that the [[Corporate Alliance/Legends|Corporate Alliance]] and the [[Commerce Guild/Legends|Commerce Guild]] were allying themselves with separatist movement head [[Count of Serenno/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]], before being cut off by an attacking [[Droideka/Legends|droideka]].<ref name="AotC" /> Coorr and everyone else present in the room realized that this was a moment of great importance.<ref name="Insider 60" />  

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|I had the misfortune to spend a solid 12 minutes chatting with Senator Ronet Coorr. His droning about redirecting arterial traffic through industrial sectors left me wanting to open my arterial traffic on those laughably dated drapes. But I digress.|Dyslogia Twang on Ronet Coorr|<ref name="HoloNet News Vol 531 53" />}}
Coorr had [[Color/Legends|light]] skin, brown [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], and brown [[Hair/Legends|hair]].<ref name="DB" /> Dyslogia Twang described the senator as droning and their talk misfortunate.<ref name="HoloNet News Vol 531 53" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Ronet Coorr.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Ronet Coorr was created for Episode II.]]
Ronet Coorr was created for<ref name="DB" /> the [[prequel trilogy]] film [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']],<ref name="AotC">{{Film|II}}</ref> which released on [[May 16]], [[2002]].<ref name="Year">''[[Star Wars Year by Year: A Visual Chronicle]]''</ref> Early production notes for the film referred to Coorr as "Male Senator #2."<ref name="DB" /> Coorr was first mentioned in the online article "Palpatine forms Loyalist Committee," published in [[HoloNet News Vol. 531 #49]], part of the [[HoloNet News (original website)|HoloNet News]] website. The news issue,<ref name="HoloNet News Vol 531 49" /> written by [[Paul Ens]] and [[Pablo Hidalgo]],<ref name="Credits">{{HoloNetNewsWeb||credits.html|Credits}}</ref> was released on [[March 28]], 2002.<ref name="HoloNet News Vol 531 49">{{HoloNetNewsWeb|49|news/13328_1.html|Palpatine forms Loyalist Committee}}</ref> Coorr was first identified as the character appearing in Episode II<ref name="DB" /> on [[May 17]], 2002<ref name="Pillars">{{SWArchive|url=databank/news/2002/05/news20020517.html|text=Pillars of the Old Republic}}</ref> with the character's [[StarWars.com]] [[Databank (original)|Databank]] entry.<ref name="DB" />

A story involving Coorr and [[Onaconda Farr/Legends|Onaconda Farr]] dealing with their resignation from the Senate was later overwritten by the events of the ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]'' television series, which saw Farr killed off while still a member of the Senate.

===Non-canon content===
In [[21 BBY/Legends|21 BBY]], during [[Operation Durge's Lance]], Coorr was bribed by fellow Committee senator [[Onaconda Farr/Legends|Onaconda Farr]] to redirect the [[Republic Navy/Legends|Naval]] fleet at Iseno to Farr's native [[Rodia/Legends|Rodia]]. As the Iseno fleet was meant to reinforce the planet [[Duro/Legends|Duro]], the fleet transfer served to quicken the [[Battle of Duro (Clone Wars)|fall of that planet]] to the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy]]. Following the battle, when news of the bribe became known, both Coorr and Farr resigned from the Senate, and returned to their homeworlds.
